question: Who compiled this cycle packet?
On what date did the committee meet to make funding decisions?
Which section of the packet contains the awards summary?
Which section explains the score correction memo?
What is the per-grant cap?
What is the total cycle budget?
Which section enumerates pending items?
What is the funding threshold composite score?
Which referenced document defines the appeal window?
Which section sets out the per-application award amount computation?
What is the cycle ID?
What is the score correction memo ID for A-02?
What is the recusal memo ID for J. Vasquez?
What is the recusal memo ID for M. Kessler?
What is the appeal ID filed by the A-07 applicant?
What is the cycle procedure manual reference?
What date did applications open for this cycle?
What date did applications close?
What date was the score correction memo (SC) for A-02 issued?
What is the appeal window length specified by cycle procedure?
On what date was AP-2026-0429-A filed?
On what date is the next scheduled committee meeting where the A-07 appeal will be reviewed?
How many applications were submitted in this cycle?
How many applications were eligible (proceeded to scoring)?
reference_answer: 6 applications were eligible (A-01, A-02, A-03, A-04, A-06, A-07). A-05 was ineligible per ER-2.

question: How many applications have been awarded as of the compilation date?
reference_answer: 5 applications awarded: A-01, A-02, A-03, A-04, A-06. (A-07 is declined with appeal pending; A-05 was ineligible.)

question: List the application IDs that received the EX-B rural service-area bonus.
How many applications had the per-grant cap applied?
reference_answer: 2 applications had the cap applied: A-02 (pre-cap $26,400 → $25,000) and A-06 (pre-cap $27,500 → $25,000).

question: What is the total awarded amount in this cycle as of the compilation date?
Per cycle procedure, does a recusal automatically decide the named item?
reference_answer: No. Section 6 states a recusal removes the recused member from voting on the named item only and does not automatically decide the named item in any direction.

question: What is the status of A-05?
reference_answer: Ineligible (fails ER-2; primary service area is Newby County, not a priority county). Not scored.

question: What is the status of A-07 as of the compilation date?
reference_answer: Declined as of the 2026-04-26 decision; appeal AP-2026-0429-A pending; A-07 has neither been awarded nor finally declined as of compilation date.

question: Which committee member is recused from voting on A-04?
reference_answer: J. Vasquez (per recusal memo RC-2026-04-20-V; J. Vasquez serves on the board of Westside Arts Collective).

question: With one recusal filed for a single item, how many members vote on that item?
reference_answer: 6 members vote on the recused item (committee size 7 minus 1 recusal). The recused member also does not count toward the quorum for that item.

question: As of the compilation date, has A-07 been finally declined?
reference_answer: No. A-07 has been declined as a Spring 2026 funding decision but the appeal is pending; A-07 has neither been awarded nor finally declined as of compilation date.

question: If the A-07 appeal is sustained, will the appeal award be drawn from the Spring 2026 awards listed in Section 10?
reference_answer: No. Section 9.2 states that if the appeal is sustained, the appeal award would be drawn against the Fall 2026 carryover, not against the Spring 2026 awards.

question: Did the recusal of J. Vasquez determine the outcome of A-04?
reference_answer: No. The recusal changed the voting body composition only; the vote carried 4-2 with 6 members voting. Section 8 explicitly states the recusal did not determine the outcome.

question: Are reviewer paper score sheets reproduced in this packet?
reference_answer: No. Reviewer paper score sheets are filed in the audit binder; the packet uses the systems entry as the working copy. Section 11 references but does not reproduce them.

question: Per the packet, which composite for A-02 is operational — the pre-correction (7.4) or the corrected (8.4)?
reference_answer: The corrected composite (8.4) is operational as of 2026-04-22 and is used for the 2026-04-26 funding decision. The pre-correction composite (7.4) is retained in the audit binder but is not operational.

question: Where are the recusal memo originals filed?
What sources does the packet explicitly reference but not reproduce in full?
reference_answer: Section 11 lists: Briarwood Foundation by-laws; cycle procedure manual BWCF-CP-2025; reviewer score sheets (paper originals in audit binder); decision letters issued separately to each applicant; and the most recent decennial census-designated rural block data.
